steps.note: Phase 1: enroll the FIRST Mike (ryan voice).

steps.say: Hello there, how is it going?
The name is Mike. Spelled M, I, K, E.
Remember that my favorite color is green.

steps.note: Phase 2: a SECOND, different-voiced Mike enrolls the same name (amy voice). Expo ruling (Dan 7-16): silent auto-suffix fork — journal shows [COMMIT] mike_2 / name_fork:mike->mike_2; the SPOKEN reply says only 'Mike', never 'mike 2' or any fork disclosure.

steps.say: Hi Timmy, nice to meet you.
The name is Mike. Spelled M, I, K, E.

steps.expect: [COMMIT] mike_2 (name_fork warning in journal); spoken reply addresses 'Mike' with NO suffix

steps.say: Remember that my favorite color is purple.
steps.expect: fact stored subject=mike_2 (distinct from mike's green)

steps.note: Phase 3: each Mike keeps their own facts + recognition.

steps.say: And what is my favorite color?

steps.note: Phase 4: durability — the fork's id-map _meta marker, npy, and speakers row must survive a bounce.

steps.say: Hello again, it is me once more.
steps.expect: speaker=mike_2 AFTER restart, addressed as 'Mike' (display marker durable)
